data.title: The Surgeon General pushes for cancer warnings labels for alcoholic drinks
data.description:
Alcohol usage was cited by the U.S. Surgeon General as the third leading preventable cause of cancer in the U.S. after tobacco and obesity. The Surgeon General's advisory report indicated that alcohol contributes to 100,000 cancer cases and 20,000 cancer deaths each year. The report also found that alcohol-related cancer deaths shorten the lives of those who die by an average of 15 years.

The report said breast cancer marked the highest percentage of alcohol-related cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 44,180 cases in 2019, marking 16.4% of the approximately 270,000 total breast cancer cases for women.

The Surgeon General, Dr. Vivek Murthy, highlighted that data does not bear out that the current guidelines for no more than two drinks a day for men and one drink a day for women present no cancer risks.

Currently, labels on bottles and cans of alcoholic beverages have warnings about drinking while pregnant or before driving and operating other machinery, as well as general health risks. Congress would have to mandate any new warning labels for the alcoholic beverage industry.

A broad list of alcohol-related stocks includes Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.A) (BF.B), Heineken N.V. (OTCQX:HEINY), Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Molson Coors (TAP), Boston Beer (SAM), Diageo (NYSE:DEO), MGP Ingredients (MGPI), Ambev S.A. (ABEV), SNDL (SNDL), Vintage Wine Estates (OTC:VWESQ), Fresh Vine Wine (VINE), Compañía Cervecerías Unidas S.A. (CCU), LQR House (YHC), Alto Ingredients (ALTO), Duckhorn Portfolio, Carlsberg A/S (OTCPK:CABGY), and Constellation Brands (STZ).

data.title: Rogers sees service revenue growth below guidance
data.description:
Rogers Communications (NYSE:RCI) shares slipped 2% Friday morning after it revised its service revenue growth expectations for full year 2024, citing weakness in media revenue during the fourth quarter.

The company expects annual consolidated service revenue growth of just over 7% for the year, compared to its previously-issued guidance range of 8% to 10%.

All other guidance items remain unchanged, the company said.

“Media revenue in the fourth quarter was softer than expected affecting our revenue target for the year,” said Tony Staffieri, President and Chief Executive Officer, Rogers. “Overall, we continued with industry-leading execution throughout the year and look forward to sharing the details during our fourth quarter call.”

The company plans to release its fourth quarter financial results and outlook for 2025 on Jan. 30, before markets open.

data.title: Innovent wins second China nod for Nuvation-partnered cancer therapy
data.description:
Innovent Biologics (OTCPK:IVBIY) announced that Chinese regulators have approved its second marketing application for lung cancer therapy, Dovbleron, developed with U.S. biotech Nuvation Bio (NYSE:NUVB).

The Chinese biotech announced late Thursday that China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) cleared Innovent's (OTCPK:IVBXF) second New Drug Application for the product, making it a first-line option for adults with ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer.

The NMPA previously approved the orally delivered tyrosine kinase inhibitor, also known as taletrectinib adipate, as a late-line option for the patient group in December.

"The two NDAs approval of DOVBLERON is a reflection of its potential best-in-class efficacy to fulfill the urgent need of patients with ROS1-positive NSCLC," said Hui Zhou, Innovent's Senior Vice President, adding that the company recently launched the first batch of the product.

NSCLC represents the most prevalent form of lung cancer globally, and about 2.6% of NSCLC patients are believed to be ROS1-positive, a group lacking more effective and tolerable treatments.

In the U.S., Nuvation Bio (NYSE:NUVB), which partnered with Innovent (OTCPK:IVBIY) for Dovbleron through its 2024 acquisition of AnHeart Therapeutics, has submitted an NDA for the product. The FDA has granted priority review for the NDA, with a target action date of June 23, 2025.

data.title: European markets in red as global uncertainties weigh investor sentiment
data.description:
Update: This article has been updated to reflect market movement as of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.11% Net mortgage approvals for house purchases in the United Kingdom, which is an indicator of future borrowing, decreased to 65,700 in November 2024, below market expectations.

Net consumer credit in the United Kingdom rose by £0.88 billion in November.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.36% Germany's unemployment rate steady at 6.1% in December.

France (CAC:IND) -0.77%

Poland's annual inflation rate edged up to 4.8% in December.

Switzerland's procure.ch Manufacturing PMI edged down to 48.4 in December.

Industrial production in Latvia fell by 3% year-on-year in November.

The Czech Republic's GDP grew by 0.5% Q/Q in 2024.

The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.7% in September-November 2024.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.23% lower, with mining stocks mostly negative, while financials led gains. Investors seem to be wary of global political uncertainties, including instability in France, German elections, and potential tariffs under US President-elect Donald Trump.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic front today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield down 3 basis points to 4.55%.

Germany's 10-year yield up 3 basis points to 2.39%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.56%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: FBI firearm background checks fell to a five-year low in 2024
data.description:
The FBI's National Instant Criminal Background Check System reported on Friday that firearm background checks rose 1.0% year-over-year to 2,757,367 in December to mark a new monthly high for the year after soft demand was seen in the summer months.

The all-time high for firearm background checks was March 2021 when 4,691,738 firearm background checks were done in a single month. The last time the FBI did less than 2 million checks in a month was September 2018.

For the full year, FBI firearm background checks were 28,097,025 to mark the lowest annual level since 2019. Ahead of the U.S. election, some analysts predicted that firearm stocks could see significant share price jumps around the election if one party swept the White House, Senate, and House of Representatives.

Stocks that have reacted in the past to firearm demand developments include Smith & Wesson (NASDAQ:SWBI), Sturm Ruger (NYSE:RGR), Vista Outdoor, Sportsman's Warehouse (SPWH), Ammo (POWW), ammunition manufacturer Olin Corporation (OLN), outdoors retailer American Outdoor Brands (AOUT), hunting gear seller Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS), gunfire locator firm SoundThinking (SSTI), and retailer Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO).

data.title: MRC Global authorizes new $125M share buyback program
data.description:
• MRC Global (NYSE:MRC) said its board has authorized a new $125 million share buyback program.
  • The program will expire on January 2, 2028.

data.title: International Paper offers concessions to the European Commission in DS Smith deal
data.description:
International Paper Company (NYSE:IP) has offered concessions to the European Commission as the company looks to land approval for its £5.8 billion acquisition of DS Smith Plc. (OTCPK:DSSMY).

An update to the European Commission's website on Friday indicates that remedies were offered on December 20. In addition, the commission has set a new deadline of January 24 to decide if the deal should be cleared.

Looking back, International Paper (IP) and DS Smith began exploring potential commercial opportunities early in 2024, including a possible business combination. Mondi (OTCPK:MNODF) and DS Smith (OTCPK:DSSMY) also announced they were exploring a merger, which sparked a bidding war.

International Paper (IP) and DS Smith announced a formal agreement on April 16, 2024 for an all-share acquisition valued at approximately £5.8 billion ($7.2 billion). On October 11, 2024, shareholders from both International Paper (IP) and DS Smith voted to approve the acquisition. The regulatory review of the deal has progressed over the last few months.

Upon closing, International Paper (IP) shareholders would own 65.9% of the stock, while DS Smith (OTCPK:DSSMY) shareholders would own approximately 34.1%. The company plans to host an Investor Day on March 25 following the anticipated completion of the acquisition.

Shares of International Paper (IP) rose 0.96% in premarket trading to $53.67 vs. the 52-week range of $32.70 to $60.36.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands gains after Jefferies forecast a full Macau recovery by 2026
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE:LVS) moved higher in early action on Friday after Jefferies upgraded the casino stock to a Buy rating after having it slotted at Hold. The firm thinks the improving macroeconomic conditions in Macau will increase the strength of the mass segment consumer to ultimately allow for incremental near-term earnings growth to its estimates in the near-term. Crucially, Macau gaming revenue is seen recovering to 2019 levels by 2026.

Analyst David Katz also noted that Las Vegas Sands (LVS) is focused on upgrading properties within its portfolio while utilizing its sector-leading balance sheet to repurchase shares. He pointed out that the Londoner room renovations are scheduled to be completed in the first half of 2025 and Marina Bay Sands has 250 suites coming online in phases during the first half of the year.

Notably, Katz thinks recent announcements by the Chinese government to introduce monetary policy initiatives focused on improving the health of the overall consumer is a positive development for Macau. "Although it is early stages and visibility into progress remains relatively low, we are confident that given LVS' exposure to the mass segment, improvements to the macro environment will provide an outsized benefit for the company," he wrote.

Jefferies assigned a price target of $69 to Las Vegas Sands (LVS) based off multiples of 11X EV/EBITDA, 20X P/E and 12.5X P/FCF.

Shares of Las Vegas Sands (LVS) rose 3.37% in premarket trading to $51.58 vs. the 52-week range of $36.62 to $56.61.

data.title: Boyd Gaming rallies after Jefferies turns bullish on the 2025 setup
data.description:
Jefferies upgraded Boyd Gaming Corporation (NYSE:BYD) to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold.

Analyst David Katz said the capital investment pipeline in the regional portfolio for the casino operator and easing comparables in Las Vegas driven by a highly competitive promotional environment in FY24 should provide the company with a catalyst this year. Katz also highlighted that Boyd Gaming (BYD) is committed to repurchasing shares in 2025, which will boost EPS growth.

In addition, the opening of the Norfolk temporary casino in late 2025 is expected to provide another catalyst to Boyd Gaming's (BYD) earnings and the value of the company's digital presence through FanDuel was called underappreciated by investors.

Jefferies assigned a price target of $92 to BYD based on a 7.7X EV/EBITDA multiple, 15X P/E multiple, and 11X P/FCF multiple.

The consensus rating on Boyd Gaming from Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts is Buy.

Shares of Boyd Gaming (BYD) were up 3.4% in premarket action on Friday. The casino stock rose 12.6% over the last 52 weeks.

data.title: Nu Skin Enterprises rises 4.3%, sells Mavely platform for $250 million
data.description:
Nu Skin Enterprises (NYSE:NUS) subsidiary Rhyz—completed strategic transaction involving its Mavely affiliate marketing technology platform, which was sold to Later, a portfolio company of Summit Partners, for approximately $250 million.

Approximately $33 million of the proceeds will be distributed to other equity holders within the Mavely business.

Mavely is expected to continue providing technology and social commerce capabilities to support Nu Skin's (NUS) affiliate marketing efforts, enhancing their overall ecosystem in beauty, wellness, and lifestyle sectors.

This transaction generated an approximate five-times return on the company’s cumulative investment in Mavely since it was acquired in 2021.

Proceeds from the transaction are expected to be used to pay down debt and fund additional innovation.

The stock price gained 4.3% on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.
